content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Let AAA be an N√óNN \times NN√óN grid such that Ai,j=i+N‚čÖ(j‚ąí1)A_{i, j} = i + N\cdot(j-1)Ai,j‚Äč=i+N‚čÖ(j‚ąí1) for 1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N1 \leq i, j \leq N1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N. For example, if N=4N = 4N=4 the grid looks like:



You start at the top left corner of the grid, i.e, cell (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1). You would like to reach the bottom-right corner, cell (N,N)(N, N)(N,N). To do so, whenever you are at cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), you can move to either cell (i+1,j)(i+1, j)(i+1,j) or cell (i,j+1)(i, j+1)(i,j+1) provided that the corresponding cell lies within the grid (more informally, you can make one step down or one step right).

The score of a path you take to reach (N,N)(N, N)(N,N) is the sum of all the numbers on that path.

You are given an integer KKK that is either 000 or 111. Is there a path reaching (N,N)(N, N)(N,N) such that the parity of its score is KKK?

Recall that the parity of an integer is the (non-negative) remainder obtained when dividing it by 222. For example, the parity of 246246246 is 000 and the parity of 111111 is 111. In other words, an even number has parity 000 and an odd number has parity 111.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format

For each test case, output the answer on a new line ‚ÄĒ `YES` if such a path exists, and `NO` otherwise.

Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, and `yes` will all be treated as identical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one possible path, which starts and ends at the same cell. The score of this path is 111, which is odd. An even score is impossible.
